sanskrit: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन । सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन  केवल कर्तव्यमात्र करना है  ऐसा समझकर जो नियत कर्म आसक्ति और फलका त्याग करके किया जाता है, वही सात्त्विक त्याग माना गया है ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna, when fixed duty is done as only duty, without clinging to results, that renunciation is called sattvic.
essence: Renunciation means doing what must be done without gripping the result.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Fixed duty becomes pure renunciation when it is done without attachment to personal gain or to the result.
simpleInsight: You can stay committed without becoming needy about what comes back.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still caught between action and withdrawal. Krishna now refines renunciation: not abandoning duty, but doing the required work with no grasping for reward. That is the cleanest form of letting go.
modernRelevance: You send the message that could change everything, then stare at the screen for the reply. This verse says the honesty was the task; the response was never yours to hold.
translationLiteral: कार्यम् (to be done) / इति एव (thus only) / यत् (which) / कर्म (action) / नियतम् (fixed, prescribed) / क्रियते (is done) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / सङ्गम् (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / फलम् (result, fruit) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सः (that) / त्यागः (renunciation) / सात्त्विकः (sattvic) / मतः (is considered)
insight: It shifts action from reward-chasing to clean responsibility.

quoteVariants.text: Do your duty fully. Release the result.
The work is yours; the result is not.
Do the duty. Drop the outcome.
